notes: This lo-fi album cover is a deceptively hard perceptual task: the ceiling is bathed in a vivid cosmic-style color gradient that several models misread as an actual starry night sky. Models that correctly identified the surface as a ceiling (gemma4-12b/26b/31b, qwen3.5-4b, qwen3.5-9b) scored highest.

Every model transcribed the title text 'songs to stare at the ceiling to' verbatim and correctly noted the absence of people — text extraction is uniformly strong on this image. No model suffered from phrase-repetition failure (the phrase appears once and all models reported it once).

The most severe failure was gemma4-e4b|think, which hallucinated an emoji ('😫') appended to the verbatim title text. Injecting a fabricated character into the most important text string is a critical OCR failure.

Scene-misidentification failures (glm-4.6v-flash-9b, qwen3-vl-8b, qwen3.6-27b|think, qwen3.6-35b-a3b|think) all shared a common pattern: describing an exterior building/sky view rather than the interior ceiling, and/or calling dust grain 'stars'.

Common perceptual hallucinations across mid-tier models: fabricated raindrops/condensation on the window glass (qwen3.5-9b|nothink, qwen3.6-27b|nothink, qwen3.6-35b-a3b|nothink), invented 'bare tree branches' or 'snow' outside the window (qwen3.5-4b|nothink/think), and unsubstantiated lamp shapes like 'cobra-head' or 'hexagonal'.

Gemma4-26b-a4b|think and gemma4-31b|think were the only models to deliver both verbatim text AND a fully accurate scene interpretation (ceiling as primary subject, interior perspective, album-cover aesthetic) without any hallucinated details.
